text: pulled, tearing the synthetic skin away from his jaw, revealing the metal plates beneath. He snapped his head to the side, ripping off more skin before her grip broke. When he turned his gaze on her, she spat in his face . Warlord’s open palm cracked against the side of her face. Blood sprayed from her mouth, gathering on grass and dirt in bright droplets. He slapped her again before she recovered . Ronin twisted beneath the gearheads, shifting his weight to throw them off-balance. Lara would be killed while he watched. He would never have her back, would never be able to touch her again or hear her voice directly, would never see the spark of life in her eyes or experience the surge of emotion roused by her dances . He pulled one of his arms free and latched it on the throat of the nearest gearhead. Metal crunched beneath his closing fingers, and he pulled. The bot’s head lolled back limply as Ronin tossed away the components of its neck . Some of the weight pinning him fell away, and he pushed himself up . Four shots rang out, echoing like thunder in the morning sky. Four points of pain exploded across Ronin’s torso. He fell to the ground on his stomach, systems reeling as they assessed the damage, alerts blaring across his processors . “Ronin!” Lara cried out, voice weak and strained . “Pull his fucking power cell,” Warlord said. “Let the Dust have him .” Gearheads pinned down his limbs, and new pain flared down Ronin’s back, sensory circuits broken as his skin was torn away. He registered strong force exerted on his casing, and a moment later an armored plate was pried loose . There was a crack, and Lara screamed. Ronin lifted his head, twisting his neck to see her curled in the dirt, blood trickling from her nose and mouth . “Lara!” he shouted . A hand clamped over his power cell . Ronin ceased to exist . * * * S omething flickered through the blackness, a spark of energy in a field of nothing. It pulsed across electrodes, rebooting systems one-by-one in its wake, spreading power throughout Ronin’s body. Diagnostics indicated five breaches of his casing —four on his back, and an exit hole on his chest . He opened his eyelids, but it took three seconds for his optics to come online. At first, there was only bright white, but a shadowy figure materialized as the cameras adjusted. Static hummed through the image, and finally his vision cleared . Newton was kneeling over Ronin, his mouth moving . Ronin’s audio receptors crackled on next, varying wildly between a distant, unintelligible drone and the overwhelming feedback from the wind before finally normalizing to reasonable levels . “—need to get up, Ronin!” Newton pleaded . Ronin nodded. The ground was no place for him, especially with his casing breached. But why was Newton so anxious about it? With Newton’s help, he rose into a sitting position, processors reviewing various data as it became available . This had happened before, though Ronin hadn’t known Newton’s name, then. Hadn’t claimed a name of his own . He accessed his most recent memories as systems powered up. He’d been traveling through the Dust, seeking shelter from a storm, and Lara — Pull his fucking energy cell . Ronin surged to his feet as the memories assailed him, every instant captured with unerring, terrifying detail . “Where is she?” he demanded, turning his head to search the immediate area. The only evidence of Warlord’s presence were the boot prints in the dirt. All of Ronin’s gear — rifle, packs of scrap, water, food, and ammunition — was gone . Newton adjusted his position, blocking Ronin’s view. “You will need to remain calm. There was nothing — ” Ronin shoved Newton aside and stumbled forward, falling to his knees beside the figure on the ground. Dark blood had splattered the dust beneath her, more of it drying in her hair and on her clothes. Her face was turned away . He reached forward to touch her arm, stopping when he saw the dark bruising around her elbow. “Lara,” he said, and her name was carried away on the wind . “She’s not dead. Not yet,” Newton said. “But…her arm is broken, her ribs are, at the very least, fractured, and she’s bruised in large patches of her torso and face. She could recover from all that, with proper and immediate treatment, but if she’s bleeding internally, it will kill her .” “You can diagnose her,” Ronin said, his voice flat, not looking away from Lara, “you can save her .” “Not I. I have some of the knowledge required, but none of the tools .” Gently, Ronin hooked her hair with his fingers and drew it away from her face. Her eyes were black and swollen shut, lips split and oozing blood. Weak breaths struggled through her open mouth . He could hardly recognize the woman he knew. The woman he loved . His hands fell to his sides, fingers curling to dig grooves in the dirt. He’d long expected to meet his end in the Dust, one day, but it wasn’t supposed to take her . Clenching his fists, he slammed them into the ground, again and again. The Dust had provided, and he’d walked it without complaint, taking the hardships as they came. It was his means of survival. His only purpose . It had no right to demand this price . He’d vowed to protect her , to — This was the world Newton had woken him up to. The first time, so long ago, Ronin had lost himself upon waking. Now, he had lost the only person he cared about in all the years since. Newton had reactivated him, but Lara had granted Ronin life . Was the bot standing behind him worthy of some of Ronin’s newfound hatred ? “I wish
